    1. handle(RequestParser.RequestInfo, OutputStream)
    2. close()

Interface Servlet

All Known Implementing Classes:
ConfLoader, HtmlLoader, TopicDisplayer
public interface Servlet
Core extension point for handling HTTP requests in the server framework.

A Servlet receives a parsed RequestParser.RequestInfo and the client's OutputStream. It is responsible for writing a complete HTTP/1.1 response, including the status line, headers, the blank line separator (\r\n\r\n), and the response body.

Servlets are registered on an HTTPServer via HTTPServer.addServlet(java.lang.String, java.lang.String, servlets.Servlet). When a matching request arrives, the server invokes handle(server.RequestParser.RequestInfo, java.io.OutputStream) on a worker thread from its ExecutorService thread pool.

Thread safety: The server does not serialize calls to the same servlet instance. If a servlet holds shared mutable state, it must synchronize access or use concurrent data structures. Stateless servlets are inherently safe for concurrent use.

Example custom servlet:


 public class EchoServlet implements Servlet {
     public void handle(RequestParser.RequestInfo ri, OutputStream out) throws IOException {
         byte[] body = ("Echo: " + ri.getUri()).getBytes();
         out.write(("HTTP/1.1 200 OK\r\nContent-Length: " + body.length + "\r\n\r\n").getBytes());
         out.write(body);
         out.flush();
     }
     public void close() throws IOException { }
 }
See Also:

  • MyHTTPServer
  • RequestParser.RequestInfo

  • Method Summary

    Modifier and Type
    Method
    Description
    void
    close()
    Releases any resources held by this servlet.
    void
    handle(RequestParser.RequestInfo ri, OutputStream toClient)
    Handles an incoming HTTP request and writes the response to the client.

  • Method Details

    • handle

      void handle(RequestParser.RequestInfo ri, OutputStream toClient) throws IOException
      Handles an incoming HTTP request and writes the response to the client.

      The implementation must write a full HTTP/1.1 response to toClient, including status line, headers, and body. The server closes the client socket after this method returns.

      Parameters:
      ri - the parsed request information (method, URI, parameters, body)
      toClient - the output stream connected to the client socket
      Throws:
      IOException - if an I/O error occurs while writing the response

    • close

      void close() throws IOException
      Releases any resources held by this servlet.

      Called once per registered servlet instance during HTTPServer.close(). Implementations that do not hold resources may provide an empty body.

      Throws:
      IOException - if an I/O error occurs while closing resources
